title: THE MOUNTAIN REMEMBERS OUR STEPS (2025)
author: བདེ་སྐྱིད་སྒྲོལ་མ།, Bde skyid sgrol ma
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This footage captures a profound journey. Setting out from Kunming, three close friends traveled thousands of miles across the Tibetan Plateau, eventually arriving at sacred Mount Kailash in Ngari. This video carries no religious propaganda or sermonizing. It is a faithful record of a beautiful time shared among friends, and a tribute to the deep connection we felt with the land. Through our lens, we documented gravel paths, fluttering prayer flags, and timeless pilgrimage tradition. For local Tibetans, circumambulating Mount Kailash, a grueling, sacred 52-kilometer trek, is regarded as one of the most significant spiritual journeys of their lives. We hope this raw, peaceful, and authentic documentary brings you our experience of purity, serenity, and  the breathtaking beauty of the Plateau's wilderness.

keywords: Tibetan mountain pilgrimage, Tibetan mountain circumambulation,
